issues.description: JSON parsing is fragile - uses strstr() naive string searching which could break with different JSON formatting or if values contain the search patterns
Unicode escape sequences in JSON strings are skipped rather than properly handled in unescape_json_string
Terminal history/scrollback is not persisted - only workspace metadata is saved (name, cwd, git_branch). This was documented as left undone in the handoff.
No validation that restored workspace IDs don't conflict with existing ones when session is loaded
If g_get_home_dir() returns NULL, fallback to "." may not be a valid directory

sharedStateObservations.description: The backend-worker skill (referenced in features.json) was not reviewed as the SKILL.md file was not found in .factory directory
Transcript skeleton shows 0 tool calls - the worker may have used a different execution model or the transcript was not captured properly

notes: The implementation fulfills the core requirement of VAL-CROSS-003: Session Persistence. The feature saves workspace layout (id, name, cwd, git_branch, notification_count) to ~/.config/cmux/session.json on exit and restores it on startup. Key strengths: (1) proper XDG_CONFIG_HOME fallback, (2) config directory creation with parents, (3) basic JSON escaping for special characters, (4) cwd validation with fallback on restore, (5) proper active workspace restoration with fallback. Known limitations: (1) fragile JSON parsing that would break with reordered fields or complex values, (2) no terminal content/scrollback preservation, (3) no tab state preservation. The handoff documented these as intentional limitations. Build compiles successfully and unit tests pass.
